styleEditor.title: Style Editor
styleEditor.subtitle: Visual classes for SVG pictograms
styleEditor.restoreConfirm: This will replace all styles and keyframes with the built-in defaults. Continue?

library.structureRequiresRaw: Structured form is generated from the traced SVG. Vectorise first.

home.description: Step-by-step generative system for pictograms from communicative intent to visual representation
home.icapDescription: The ICAP-50 corpus represents a set of 50 phrases organized into 8 pragmatic categories: Request, Reject, Direct, Accept, Social, Emotion, Comment, and Question.
home.importDescription: Define your own vocabulary from custom phrases. Upload a .txt file with one phrase per line.
home.loadLibraryWarning: You have {count} pictogram(s) in your library. Loading a new library will overwrite your current work. Do you want to continue?
home.exampleLibrariesDescription: Example libraries showcasing different graphic styles
home.emptyLibraryHint: Type a phrase to turn it into a pictogram
home.emptyLibraryImport: You can also import a list of phrases
home.emptyLibraryImportTooltip: .txt file with one phrase per line

evaluation.errorLoading: Could not load evaluation guide. Please check your connection.

descriptions.clarity: The degree to which the pictogram is visually clear, legible and free from visual ambiguity
descriptions.recognizability: The ease with which a viewer can identify what the pictogram represents
descriptions.semantic_transparency: The degree to which the pictogram accurately conveys the specific meaning of the phrase
descriptions.pragmatic_fit: The usefulness and appropriateness of the pictogram in real-world AAC contexts
descriptions.cultural_adequacy: The appropriateness and relevance of the pictogram for the cultural and linguistic context
descriptions.cognitive_accessibility: The accessibility and usability of the pictogram for users with cognitive differences

config.spaceNameTooltip: Identify your pictogram library or collection. This name will appear as the title on the home page.
config.creditsTooltip: People or institution responsible for this library. Included in metadata when sharing or exporting to ensure proper attribution.
config.creditsPlaceholder: E.g.: Herbert Spencer — e[ad] PUCV / Accessibility & Inclusion Unit
config.licenseTooltip: License applied to derivative work from this library. Gemini models do not impose restrictions on commercial use if terms of use are respected.
config.geoContextTooltip: NLU processing language and geographic reference region. Affects cultural interpretation of pictograms.
config.visualStylePromptTooltip: Graphic style description that Gemini will use to render all pictograms in this space.
config.imageModelTooltip: NanoBanana Flash is faster; NanoBanana Pro produces higher quality but takes longer.
config.stylesTooltip: Visual CSS style editor for generated SVGs. Define reusable classes for animations and appearance.
config.paletteColorsTooltip: Reference colours for Recraft generation. The palette guides the chromatic style of generated pictograms.
config.animationsTooltip: Disable animations and transitions in the interface. Recommended for accessibility (WCAG 2.3.3).
config.highContrastTooltip: Enable high contrast mode: reinforced borders, darker text and pure white backgrounds. Recommended for accessibility (WCAG 1.4.11).
config.recordingModificationsTooltip: When enabled, the pictogram's modification audit is recorded as it is adjusted and modified.

audit.title: Pictogram audit
audit.openTooltip: View and edit this pictogram's modification log
audit.emptyDescription: No interventions have been recorded for this pictogram.
audit.editingHint: You can edit the JSON freely. Save replaces the entire log.
audit.saveTooltip: Replace the log with the edited JSON
audit.clearTooltip: Remove all sessions and events from the log

actions.deleteAllConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete all nodes? This action cannot be undone.

messages.importError: Failed to import graph. Check the format.
messages.allCleared: All nodes and logs have been cleared.
messages.storageQuotaExceeded: ⚠️ Storage full. Export your work and delete old pictograms. Images will not be saved automatically.
messages.storageSaveFailed: ⚠️ Save failed. Export your work manually to prevent data loss.
messages.noSvgToEdit: No SVG to edit. Vectorise the image first.
messages.libraryImportWarning: Warning: Loading a library will REPLACE all current pictograms and settings (name, prompt, geolocation, etc.). Do you want to continue?

svg.traceDone: Raw tracing complete. Click Structure to apply semantic grouping.
svg.traceConverts: Converts bitmap to SVG (trace) then applies semantic structure with Gemini
svg.structureTooltip: Group paths into semantic layers using Gemini + mf-svg-schema
svg.reStructureTooltip: Re-apply semantic structure from the traced SVG

svgEditor.deleteWithChildren: Delete this element and its children?
svgEditor.noSvgHint: The SVG will appear here once loaded
svgEditor.noElementsHint: Add SVG elements to enable semantic grouping
svgEditor.selectElement: Select an element on the canvas or in the tree to edit its properties.
svgEditor.idInvalidChars: Only alphanumeric characters, hyphens and underscores
svgEditor.boolEmptyResult: Operation would produce an empty shape
svgEditor.boolUnsupported: Element type not supported by this operation
svgEditor.simplifyTooltip: Reduce nodes and refit Bezier curves (smart paths)
svgEditor.removeInlineStylesWarning: Traced colours will be replaced by CSS class values
svgEditor.convertToPathDesc: This shape must be converted to a path to edit its nodes individually.
svgEditor.notEditableShape: Select a path, polygon or line to edit nodes.
svgEditor.importSvgHint: Upload a generative SVG to begin refining

step1.title: PICTOS.net
step1.subtitle: converts a written phrase into a pictogram for communication
step1.privacyDesc: Everything you type is saved on your own device.
step1.professionalDesc: Artificial intelligence suggests; you know the context and decide.

step2.title: The Workspace
step2.subtitle: Before generating pictograms, it is advisable to prepare the space.
step2.libraryDesc: Configured once per project in the 'Library' menu. There you define a Style Prompt that ensures all generated pictograms keep the same visual identity.

step3.title: Explore the styles
step3.intro: The example libraries serve to explore how the same idea is presented in different visual styles.

step4.title: How to start
step4.subtitle: Choose how you want to begin. There is no right order.
step4.importDesc: Load a list of phrases (.txt) or exported library (.json).
step4.exploreDesc: Open example libraries to see results.
step4.createDesc: Type a phrase in the search bar and generate a pictogram.

share.requiresEvaluation: Evaluation required to share the pictogram
share.lowScore: The pictogram must have an average evaluation ≥ 4.0 to share
share.alreadyShared: This pictogram has already been shared with PICTOS
share.noToken: GitHub token not configured. Define GITHUB_TOKEN in environment variables.
share.thanksMessage: Thank you for sharing your pictogram with PICTOS. This pictogram will be visible to the entire community.

quota.title: Daily limit reached
quota.message: You have generated {count} pictograms today, which is the maximum allowed for project participants. The limit resets at midnight UTC.
quota.contact: If you need additional access, please contact the researcher:

footer.researchDesc: PICTOS.net is a design research project on generative pictograms for augmentative and alternative communication (AAC). Developed as part of doctoral research on cognitive accessibility.
footer.collaborateDesc: Participate in the research by evaluating pictograms generated by the system.
footer.author: Herbert Spencer González
footer.affiliation1: Doctoral Researcher, Auckland University of Technology
footer.affiliation2: e[ad] School of Architecture and Design, PUCV

participate.title: Participate in the Research
participate.intro: Thank you very much for your interest in PICTOS.net! This is a research project focused on improving cognitive accessibility and exploring new ways to create generative tools for AAC.
participate.shareInstructions: If you have created a pictogram library and want to share it as an example of how the tool can be used, export the .json file and send it by email.
participate.research: You can also write to me at the same address if you are interested in participating in the study, giving feedback, or if you have ideas on how to improve these technologies.
